stories.title: The EU’s Big Tech rulebook is shifting the digital economy, says Ribera
stories.summary: The EU’s landmark tech regulations are a “success story” that are beginning to level the playing field between Silicon Valley’s giants and their digital competitors in Europe, said European Competition Commissioner Teresa Ribera on Friday. Speaking at POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum in Barcelona, Ribera said the EU’s Digital Markets Act has triggered shifts in how the European digital economy functions in the two years since the Commission began its enforcement of the bloc’s di

stories.title: ‘Irresponsible failure’: Google, Meta, Snap and Microsoft slam EU over child sexual abuse law lapse
stories.summary: Experts warn lapse could sharply reduce reports of abuse, echoing a 58% drop during a similar legal gap in 2021 Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The European parliament has blocked the extension of a law that permits big tech firms to scan for child sexual exploitation on their platforms, creating a legal gap that child safety experts say will lead to crimes going undetected. The law, which was a carve-out of the EU Privacy Act, was put in place in 20

stories.title: European army can help fix NATO, says EU defense commissioner
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU’s response to the crisis in transatlantic relations should be to build up its own military capabilities, Defense Commissioner Andrius Kubilius told POLITICO. The bloc’s defense chief was interviewed just before NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte’s Wednesday meeting with Donald Trump . Despite Rutte’s ongoing efforts to placate the U.S. president, the White House meeting ended with Trump railing at NATO allies for failing to help with the war he launched a

stories.title: 8 in 10 Europeans don’t trust US, Chinese firms with data
stories.summary: Tune in to follow the  European Pulse Forum  live from Barcelona. More than 8 in 10 Europeans don’t trust American or Chinese technology companies with their data, a new POLITICO European Pulse survey of six major EU countries showed. Around 84 percent of people said they don’t trust American tech companies to handle their personal data responsibly. Trust in Chinese tech firms is even lower among Europeans: 93 percent said they don’t trust them to be responsible with

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: ’He’s mentally unstable’: Iranian American in Congress condemns Trump’s war and pushes for his removal
stories.summary: Democratic representative Yassamin Ansari says the war has only more deeply entrenched the Iranian regime Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Donald Trump is an “evil human being” who “wants to be an emperor” and should be removed from office over the war in Iran, Yassamin Ansari , an Iranian American member of the US Congress , has told the Guardian. Ansari, the daughter of Iranian immigrants who decades ago fled the regime, spoke out after the presiden

stories.title: Hungary election campaigns enter final stretch as Orbán fights to remain in power – Europe live
stories.summary: Opposition candidate Péter Magyar warns supporters against complacency as some voters undecided ahead of Sunday Ashifa Kassam and Flora Garamvolgyi in Budapest As a child growing up in Budapest, Péter Magyar had a poster of Viktor Orbán – at the time a leading figure in the country’s pro-democracy movement – hanging above his bed. Continue reading...

stories.title: German transgender far-right extremist arrested in Czech Republic
stories.summary: German media reports say Marla-Svenja Liebich used to be a member of a neo-Nazi group called Blood and Honour.

stories.title: Who is Péter Magyar, the man leading the polls as Hungary prepares for election?
stories.summary: Former Viktor Orbán loyalist and his Tisza party have enjoyed meteoric rise as opposition movement grows As a child growing up in Budapest, Péter Magyar had a poster of Viktor Orbán – at the time a leading figure in the country’s pro-democracy movement – hanging above his bed. Orbán was one of several political figures that adorned his bedroom, Magyar told a podcast last year, hinting at his excitement over the changes sweeping the country after the collapse of communism. Now Magyar, 45, is the

stories.title: Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán, an icon for the global far right, could face defeat despite an electoral system weighted in his favour Hungarians go to the polls on 12 April in Europe’s most consequential election of the year, with Viktor Orbán, the country’s illiberal prime minister and global far-right icon, facing possible defeat, after 16 years in power, by a former loyalist, Péter Magyar. Continue reading...

stories.title: US inflation soars in March as war on Iran drives economy into uncertainty
stories.summary: Prices were up 3.3% over the year, adding to the unpredictability that first came with Trump tariffs Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox US inflation soared in March amid the US-Israel war with Iran, with prices up 0.9% compared to last month and 3.3% over the year, according to new data released Friday. The spike in the consumer price index (CPI), which measures the price of a basket of goods and services, is the largest in nearly two years and the firs

stories.title: Epstein survivors criticise Melania Trump after surprise statement – US politics live
stories.summary: First lady had called for a public hearing for survivors but a group of those affected say they have ‘done their part’ and reiterate calls for Pam Bondi to be questioned Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The annualized inflation rate has not pushed past 3% since summer 2024, when inflation was finally cooling after reaching a generational high of 9.1% in June 2022. The war on Iran has driven the American economy into deeper uncertainty, adding to a pre

stories.title: Hungary: Orban and opposition trade barbs ahead of election
stories.summary: Hungary is preparing for a potentially pivotal election, with challenger Peter Magyar and the Tisza party promising change after 16 years of Viktor Orban's rule. Polling shows nearly a quarter of voters are undecided.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough on Ukraine, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: German Bundestag chief visits Baltic states to discuss cyber defence on NATO's eastern flank
stories.summary: Klöckner will also visit the German Bundeswehr brigade in Lithuania on Thursday. Around 5,000 German soldiers are to be deployed to Lithuania by 2027 to support the local military.

stories.title: „Mi lett azzal a furcsa menyasszonnyal?”
stories.summary: Közéleti versek a Telexen! Három magyar szerzőt kértünk fel, hogy a költészet napja alkalmából írjanak verset a hazai közéletre reagálva. Másodikként Tóth Krisztina alkotását közöljük.

stories.title: Publicus Intézet: 52-39 az állás a Tisza Párt és a Fidesz között a biztos szavazó pártválasztók körében
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk 4-5 százalékon billeg, a Demokratikus Koalíció és a Magyar Kétfarkú Kutya Párt a teljes népességben és a biztos szavazó pártválasztóknál is 2-2 százalékon áll.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor bejelentette, hogy bejelent valamit, de aztán nem jelentett be semmi újat
stories.summary: Brékinget ígért, visszaszámlálás is volt, meg is szólalt, de túl sok újat nem mondott, és nem is ígért igazán semmit.

stories.title: Hungarian foreign policy is pragmatic – Szijjártó reacts to French Foreign Minister's remark about betrayal
stories.summary: The Hungarian Foreign Minister also took issue with Volodymyr Zelensky's recent visit to Transcarpathia, where he met with members of the Hungarian ethnic minority.

stories.title: Magyar Péter reagált Orbán videójára, egy dologban egyetértenek
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint is egységre van szüksége Magyarországnak.

stories.title: Tudták a véleményemet a DK-nál, szerintem tudomásul vették – mondta a Tisza jelöltjét ajánló DK-s jelölt
stories.summary: Csige Tamás, a hajdú-bihari 6-os választókerület DK-s jelöltje képviselőjelölt-társai visszalépésével nem ért egyet, szerinte akkor a jelöltséget el sem kellett volna vállalni.

stories.title: Automatikus telefonkampányt indított a Tisza
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a Telex műsorában jelentette be, hogy elindítják.

stories.title: Van, aki Fidesz-négyötödöt vár, de van, aki már ünnepi ebédet szervezett a Tisza győzelmére
stories.summary: Csütörtök este ott voltunk Magyar Péter és Orbán Viktor fórumán is, hogy utánajárjunk, az emberek hogyan élik meg a kiélezett kampányhajrát. Ami biztosan kiderült: mindkét oldal a saját győzelmében biztos, és egyik sem hiszi, hogy a másikat már bármiről meg lehetne győzni. De azért üzentek is egymásnak.

stories.title: Három képviselőjelölt ajánlóíveit foglalta le a rendőrség a vasi 2-es választókerületben
stories.summary: A független Magyar Péter ajánlóívei is a lefoglaltak között vannak.

stories.title: Szinte minden Budapestről induló és oda érkező pénteki járatát törölte a Lufthansa
stories.summary: Pénteken sztrájkolnak a légiutas-kísérők.

stories.title: „Nincs miért beleszóljak az ottani politikába” – erdélyi magyarok, akik kimaradnak a választásból
stories.summary: Ha a levélszavazatok eredményét nézzük, úgy tűnhet, az erdélyi magyarok szinte egységesen a Fideszre szavaznak. A valóságban több százezer romániai magyar tudatosan marad távol az urnáktól.

stories.title: Majka ismét posztolt a Rendszerbontó koncertről, azt kéri a résztvevőktől, ne üljenek fel semmilyen provokációnak
stories.summary: Az énekes szerint fizetett bajkeverők sodorhatják veszélybe a választás előtti koncertet.

stories.title: Több minden is arra utal, hogy tényleg omladozik a NER
stories.summary: A kampány finisében olyan történéseknek lehetünk szemtanúi, amelyekre eddig még egyáltalán nem volt példa. Rendőr, katona, extitkosszolga teregeti ki a rezsim szennyesét, és olyan helyekről szivárognak információk, amelyekről korábban elképzelhetetlen lett volna.

stories.title: Írországban autópályákat zárnak le az üzemanyagár miatt tüntetők
stories.summary: Csütörtökön a dublini reptérre is gyalogolniuk kellett az utasoknak. A rendőrség blokádként kezeli a helyzetet.

stories.title: A Trollfoci szerkesztője, Antal Adrián az MTVA Online igazgatója lett
stories.summary: Kinevezése közel fél éve megtörtént, de eddig valahogy a radar alatt maradt.

stories.title: A jó politikus inkább ne keresse az igazságot?
stories.summary: Oidipusz jó miniszterelnök lett volna. De nem lesz, a választás estéjén ugyanis kipotyognak a múlt csontvázai a szekrényből. Szophoklész drámáját Robert Icke aktualizálta 2018-ban, Magyarországon elsőként a Radnóti Színházban mutatták be 2024-ben. Idén a választás estéjén is műsorra tűzik, nem véletlenül. Kritika.

stories.title: Political Capital: Biztosból bizonytalan választás lett a Fidesz
stories.summary: A Fidesz 2010 óta minden egyes parlamenti választás előtti kampányhajrában erősödött, most ennek épp az ellenkezőjét láthatjuk. Sokan várták az utolsó két hétben a kormánypárt csodafegyverét, ami majd a javukra billenti vissza a közhangulatot, ehhez képest a fejlemények egyike sem a kormányoldalnak kedvezett. A Political Capital kampányelemzése.

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Poland's Catholic Church prepares parishes as crisis centres amid war fears
stories.summary: The Polish Bishops' Conference has established a special working group to develop an action plan in the event of an armed conflict reaching Poland, as "most Poles will first turn to the church for help, and only then to municipal institutions and offices," officials say.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Putin announces 32-hour Easter ceasefire in Ukraine
stories.summary: KYIV — Russian President Vladimir Putin announced an Orthodox Easter ceasefire in Ukraine, following repeated calls by Kyiv for a pause in hostilities over the holiday period. The ceasefire would start at 4 p.m. on Saturday and last until the end of Sunday, the day Orthodox Christians celebrate Easter, the Kremlin said in a statement late Thursday. “The commander in chief ordered a stop to military actions in all directions for that period of time,” the Kremlin said, adding that it has exp

stories.title: French bakeries and flower shops to stay open on Labor Day if new law passes
stories.summary: PARIS — Getting a fresh baguette on May 1 in France could be about to get a whole lot easier. A new law allowing vendors to remain open on Labor Day took a major step closer to passage Friday, sparking the ire of the country’s left-wing parties and powerful trade unions who say the measure undermines workers’ rights. The law would allow businesses such as bakeries, flower shops and butchers to operate without constraints on the holiday. France’s current legislation only allows

stories.title: Australian War Memorial amends Ben Roberts-Smith display after former soldier charged with war crimes
stories.summary: Text on plaque in Hall of Valour updated to include references to war crime – murder charges and the ongoing legal process Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian War Memorial has updated the display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith after the former Special Air Service (SAS) corporal was officially charged with five counts of the war crime of murder . The changes, implemented on Friday, mean nearly half of the descriptive plaque in the museum’s Hall of Valour is

stories.title: Starmer implies he didn’t tell Trump he was ‘fed up’ about his impact on rising UK energy bills – UK politics live
stories.summary: Prime minister says conversation with US president on Thursday night focused on need for ‘practical plan’ to open strait of Hormuz Tony Blair , the former Labour prime minister, has joined those saying the government should allow drilling for oil and gas in the Rosebank and Jackdaw fields in the North Sea. stories.title: UK looks to hold tech execs personally liable for intimate image abuse
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. government on Friday announced plans to hold tech company executives criminally liable if they fail to remove non-consensual intimate images from their platforms. A new government amendment, which is yet to be published but will be tabled to the Crime and Policing Bill and debated in the Commons next week, would mean senior executives who ignore media regulator Ofcom’s enforcement decisions under the Online Safety Act face imprisonment and fines. Tech Secretary Liz Kendal

stories.title: Ukraine secures oil lifeline from Gulf states in exchange for military support
stories.summary: Ukraine will receive much-needed fossil fuels from Gulf countries in exchange for the defense assistance it is providing, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said. Kyiv made defense pacts with Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates in March, but further details of those agreements were not revealed then. stories.summary: This live blog has now closed. Our coverage of the Middle East crisis continues here The UK foreign minister, Yvette Cooper , has said Lebanon must be included in any ceasefire agreement. In other remarks now being reported by Reuters, Cooper added that shipping through the strait of Hormuz must be toll-free . stories.summary: Whatever outcome of ceasefire talks, the region will have to live with a continuing threat from the regime in Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates Gulf nations will seek to add security partners as they rebuild battered economies after the US and Israel’s war on Iran and deal with an emboldened Tehran. stories.summary: Australia and Singapore will ‘make maximum efforts to meet each other’s energy security needs’ in refined fuels and LNG, according to new agreement Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s largest petrol source has pledged not to cut supplies, with Singapore’s prime minister telling Anthony Albanese that fuel will keep flowing despite the international crisis. John Feeley, a Marine helicopter pilot who later served as the US ambassador to Panama, believed Trump had been “flush wi

stories.title: Domestic abuser jailed in Scotland for killing wife who took her own life
stories.summary: Lee Milne sentenced to eight years in landmark case after being found guilty of culpable homicide of Kimberly Milne A man convicted of killing his wife, who took her own life after repeated domestic abuse, has been jailed for eight years in a case seen as a significant legal milestone. Kimberly Milne, 28, died when she jumped from a bridge in July 2023. Her estranged husband, Lee Milne, was found guilty of culpable homicide last month after a trial at the high court in Glasgow. In the UK, Samari

stories.title: Canadian mother held by ICE with daughter, 7, speaks out on families ‘suffering greatly’ in detention
stories.summary: Tania Warner and her daughter were detained in Texas facilities deemed ‘unsafe and degrading’ When Tania Warner and her seven-year-old daughter, Ayla, were released after nearly three weeks of detention by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) , the Canadian mother’s joy at regaining her freedom was tempered by the knowledge of the many families who remained incarcerated. “They were wonderful people. I just loved them and I cried so hard when I left, I just wanted to take them all with me

stories.summary: Army deployed in Pakistan’s capital as negotiations set to begin. stories.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
stories.summary: Soaring crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for president Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime , corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians. About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congre

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Vance warns Iran not to ‘play’ US as he heads to Pakistan for talks
stories.summary: Vice-president leading US delegation in negotiations due to take place in Islamabad on Saturday Netanyahu says there is no ceasefire in Lebanon as Israel launches fresh strikes The streets of Islamabad are on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepares to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East. Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious , amid Israel’s continued bombardment

stories.title: Iran war: JD Vance heads to Pakistan for talks with Tehran
stories.summary: Donald Trump has tasked his vice president JD Vance with shoring up a shaky ceasefire at the high-level talks with Iran in Islamabad. Iran has threatened to break the truce over Israel's strikes on Lebanon. DW has more.

stories.title: From the Nile to the Euphrates: Inside Israel's expansionist ambitions
stories.summary: Israel has never officially defined its borders, but Israeli settlers and ministers are flirting with the biblical idea of extending them far beyond the current state. What's behind the concept of "Greater Israel"?

stories.title: China's Xi hosts Taiwan opposition leader in Beijing
stories.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping is courting Taiwan's China-friendly opposition leader as Beijing seeks to reframe the cross-strait narrative.

stories.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
stories.summary: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

stories.title: Germany news: Protesters block Rheinmetall arms plant
stories.summary: Police have removed pro-Palestinian protesters who glued themselves to the entrance of a Berlin arms factory. Meanwhile, inflation is tracking back up as the Iran war stymied hopes that it was under control. DW has more.

stories.title: Ukrainians shot down Iran's drones in the Gulf — what does Kyiv get in return?
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president announced that Ukrainian experts in the Middle East have already shot down Iranian drones in “several countries” with domestically produced interceptors.

stories.title: Druzhba oil pipeline will be fixed this spring, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president said the Druzhba pipeline, whose operational status remains tied to EU funding for Kyiv, will be fixed later this spring, with repair work under way.

stories.title: Gaza mourns Al Jazeera reporter Mohammed Wishah killed iin Israeli drone strike
stories.summary: Gaza was mourning Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah on Thursday after he was killed in an Israeli drone strike that hit his car west of Gaza City a day earlier.

stories.title: Israel's cabinet instructed 'to begin direct negotiations' with Lebanon, Netanyahu says
stories.summary: The statement from the prime minister's office comes on the same day that the Israeli military warned residents of Beirut's southern suburbs of imminent strikes and called for them to evacuate.

stories.title: Venezuela police use tear gas against protesters demanding better pay and pensions
stories.summary: The monthly minimum wage in Venezuela is 130 bolivars (€0.23), unchanged since 2022, and roughly 330 times lower than the UN poverty line of $3 (€2.56) a day.

stories.title: Fears over West Africa's Islamist insurgency dominate Benin's election campaign
stories.summary: Cross-border raids from militant Islamists are increasingly threatening the West African state's stability.

stories.title: Kingmaker Rasmussen pauses work with Frederiksen in Danish government talks
stories.summary: Danish Foreign Minister and Moderates leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen said Friday he will not negotiate a new government with Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen — unless she talks to center-right parties about the formation of the new coalition. stories.title: Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader people on both sides of strait are Chinese in rare meeting

The meeting in Beijing between Xi and Cheng Li-wun, the chair of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), is the first such contact in a decade. Two senior negotiators for the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), the Obama-era agreement to limit Iran’s nuclear

stories.title: Not just about Gaza: the Muslim voters turning from Labour to the Greens

The taxi driver and businessman prefers to focus on community initiatives. But when the time came, he voted Labour as the lesser of two evils. Then came the war in Gaza. Continue reading...

stories.title: Who was Hilma? Af Klint exhibition to highlight exclusion of women from abstract art
stories.summary: Swedish artist, now regarded as predecessor to Kandinsky and Mondrian, died thinking world was not ready for her work The Swedish artist Hilma af Klint died believing the world was not ready for the mystical paintings that would shock the art world half a century later. The painter, now credited with pioneering the abstract art movement , did not seek recognition after peers rejected her avant garde works. Instead, she ordered they be hidden for 20 years after her death and never sold. Continue

But despite this, there is still a chance for peace.

As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance,

stories.title: International development aid suffers unprecedented 2025 dip, led by Trump gutting USAID

stories.summary: Olivia has been detained for months at the sprawling Dilley center in Texas. The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

stories.title: Argentina approves Milei’s glacier mining bill amid environmental protests
stories.summary: Legislative change backed by libertarian president makes it easier to extract metals in frozen parts of the Andes Argentina’s congress has approved a bill promoted by the libertarian president, Javier Milei, that authorises mining in ecologically sensitive areas of glaciers and permafrost, outraging environmentalists. The amendment to the “glacier law”, which was already approved by the senate in February, would make it easier to mine for metals such as copper, lithium and silver in frozen parts

stories.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

stories.title: Ceasefire offers Trump exit as Iran war becomes political liability

stories.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
stories.summary: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

stories.title: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
stories.summary: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported t

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody
stories.summary: Court reporter Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with "insulting the president." His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

stories.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China
stories.summary: Beijing’s powerbrokers are credited with winning Iran over, although one analyst says they were ‘pushing an open door’ Middle East crisis – live updates As the world struggles to make sense of what, if anything, was achieved by the ceasefire deal announced by the US and Iran on Tuesday, one major power that stands to win regardless is China. Beijing’s powerbrokers are being credited with pushing Iran towards agreeing to the ceasefire, bolstering its status as a regional mediator. In China’s tigh

stories.title: A Volkswagen-csoport visszatér az olcsó villanyautók piacára
stories.summary: A Cupra Raval alig több mint négy méter hosszú, de hatótávja akár 450 kilométer is lehet két feltöltés közt, és a csomagtartója is elég méretes.

stories.title: Fontos részlet az átjelentkezők és külképviseleten szavazók számára
stories.summary: Olvasónk hívta fel a figyelmünket egy régóta létező szabályra, amit a Nemzeti Választási Iroda is megerősített.

stories.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
stories.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
stories.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
stories.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

stories.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
stories.summary: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

stories.title: Digital detox: Offline club events in Netherlands offer screen-free social connection
stories.summary: Participants in Amsterdam and Utrecht join “offline club” events, handing in their phones to spend time on creative activities and in-person interaction.

stories.title: Railway track broke day before train accident that killed 46, Spain's Civil Guard says
stories.summary: The accident happened when a Madrid-bound service run by private firm Iryo derailed and crossed onto the opposite track, smashing into an oncoming train operated by state company Renfe.

stories.title: Multiple airstrikes hit Beirut, emergency crews respond to fires
stories.summary: Multiple Israeli strikes hit Beirut shortly after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced, sending smoke over the city.

stories.title: Former fire safety chief remains silent in Swiss bar fire probe, lawyer says
stories.summary: Prosecutors believe the fire started when champagne bottles with sparklers attached were raised too close to the ceiling in the bar's basement level, igniting the sound insulation foam.

stories.title: Camel calf Sophia born at Whipsnade Zoo, boosting conservation focus
stories.summary: New Bactrian camel calf Sophia, named after Sophia Raffles, joins the herd at Whipsnade Zoo as it celebrates 200 years and highlights vital camel conservation.

stories.title: Reports of Iran closing Strait of Hormuz 'completely unacceptable,' White House says
stories.summary: One of the major points in the two-week ceasefire agreement was that Iran would allow the resumption of shipping through Hormuz, easing a disruption in the flow of oil and gas which has sent prices soaring around the world.

stories.title: Israel ready to 'return to battle at any moment' against Iran, Netanyahu says
stories.summary: In a televised speech, Netanyahu also hit back at opposition leaders who chastised him for agreeing to the truce before Israel achieved its objectives in the war.

stories.title: Turkey puts 11 leaders of LGBTQ+ rights association on trial for 'obscenity'
stories.summary: Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but the LGBTQ+ community is frequently targeted by authorities who blame it for the country's declining birth rate.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
stories.summary: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

stories.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
stories.summary: The four astronauts said they were returning to Earth with "so many more pictures, so many more stories".

stories.title: Ceasefire threatened as Iran closes strait again and Trump warns US troops are to remain
stories.summary: In a social media post, Trump said the surge of US warships and troops will remain around Iran “until such time as the real agreement reached is fully complied with.”

stories.title: Fiscal discipline as Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership
stories.summary: Fiscal discipline will be Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership, writes Danylo Hetmantsev, chair of parliament’s finance, tax and customs committee, in an op-ed for Euronews. He argues that Ukraine has already shown it can turn shadow economy reforms into real revenue.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Beirut kill at least 182 and wound hundreds
stories.summary: Lebanon spent the night digging through rubble after Israeli strikes hit busy commercial and residential districts in central Beirut late on Wednesday, killing at least 182 people and wounding 890, according to the health ministry.

stories.title: Mexico: Miner rescued alive after 14 days trapped underground
stories.summary: After 14 days trapped in a flooded gold and silver mine, a worker has been pulled out alive while two others have died.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.summary: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: Berlin opens Süd Ost Galerie, a new open-air street art landmark
stories.summary: Germany has unveiled a new open‑air street art destination in Berlin, where the 450‑metre Süd Ost Galerie now stretches along Grenzweg in the south‑east of the capital.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon leave more than 200 dead and 1,000 wounded
stories.summary: Lebanon is searching for survivors after Israeli strikes on Beirut, Tyre and the southern village of Abbassiyeh killed at least 203 people and wounded more than 1,000, the health ministry said, as the overall death toll from attacks since early March surpasses 1,500.

stories.title: UK and Norway foil Russian submarine plot to survey undersea cables in north Atlantic
stories.summary: Britain and Norway announced new joint naval patrols aimed at protecting undersea cables from Russia in December, with a fleet of at least 13 warships protecting critical infrastructure in the North Atlantic.

stories.title: Pro-Iran groups using AI to troll Trump and try to control war narrative, analysts say
stories.summary: Analysts say the memes appear to be coming from groups linked to the government in Tehran and are part of a strategy of leveraging its limited resources to inflict damage on the US.

stories.title: Confusion reigns over Hormuz shipping reopening as Iran issues new passage conditions
stories.summary: Normal shipping in the Strait of Hormuz has not yet resumed as per the ceasefire deal, as Iran consolidates its hardline, unilateral position on lifting its blockade in apparent contradiction of the rollercoaster ceasefire deal.

stories.title: Iran rules out any restrictions on its enrichment programme, nuclear chief says
stories.summary: The issue of uranium enrichment has been central to Western relations with Iran for more than two decades, with the US and its allies accusing Tehran of seeking atomic weapons.
